Cast cobalt alloys, due to their composition, typically exhibit properties different from those of precious metal like gold. Cobalt-based materials generally offer enhanced mechanical strength and rigidity compared to gold alloys but may not share all similar physical characteristics such as ductility or elastic modulus.

The correct option is C: they do not necessarily have higher ductility than gold alloys; this would depend on specific alloy compositions and processing techniques used for both types of metals.
